title: document why sse_needs_restart defers reconnect logic out of select! arm
description: client_task.rs lines 150/187/191: the sse_needs_restart flag is set inside select! arms and checked at the top of the loop, but there is no comment explaining why the reconnect work (abort, sleep, channel recreation, re-spawn) cannot happen directly inside the arm. The reason — sse_handle and sse_rx are owned by the select! poll and cannot be mutably borrowed or dropped while select! is active — is non-obvious. A contributor first reading this will not know whether the flag is a workaround, a structural choice, or an oversight. A brief comment at the flag declaration and at each set site would make the intent clear.
